steps.title: Identify the structural signal
steps.content: The underlined portion sits after a dash following “This approach has some limits” — an explicit signal that the next phrase will name one of those limits. The function of the underlined text must therefore be to describe a specific limitation.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It describes the imprecise and subjective nature of neighborhood boundaries.’: Violates content — the underlined text says nothing about neighborhood boundaries or their subjectivity. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It emphasizes the potential utility of the team's model.’: Violates the signal — the dash-clause introduces a limitation, not a strength or use case. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘It introduces a model that a research team built to evaluate the mix of amenities in urban neighborhoods.’: Violates scope — the model was introduced in the first two sentences; the underlined portion does not re-introduce it. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘It identifies a specific flaw in using a data set about amenities in cities.’: Matches function — “Places API data tend to be restricted to customer-facing places” names exactly one concrete flaw in the data set used by the model. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
